tweets.text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

I am seeing fewer vessels transiting in general (possible with AIS off). But there's an interesting point in the video from @MarineTraffic where it is clear that the US escorted two groups of tankers through the Oman lane.

This time, they brought in 3 VLCCs.

In terms of inbound tankers, this group represented half of the total inbound and was entirely on the crude side.

Given that the Iranians have said that no technical talks will take place this week, I suspect this is another move to escalate the issue of the Oman lane.

I've included both June 28 and June 29 tables as the cutoff date might confuse some people as to why Felicity or Norita were not included on the June 29 list.
tweets.full_text:
Strait of Hormuz over the last 24 hours.

I am seeing fewer vessels transiting in general (possible with AIS off). But there's an interesting point in the video from @MarineTraffic where it is clear that the US escorted two groups of tankers through the Oman lane.

This time, they brought in 3 VLCCs.

In terms of inbound tankers, this group represented half of the total inbound and was entirely on the crude side.

Given that the Iranians have said that no technical talks will take place this week, I suspect this is another move to escalate the issue of the Oman lane.

I've included both June 28 and June 29 tables as the cutoff date might confuse some people as to why Felicity or Norita were not included on the June 29 list.

tweets.text:
$MU 

Smoked earnings last week and the same basic TA has continued to work. 

Draw the trend line, wait for price to retest + demand and then you bid. 

Assume the trend continues until it doesn't. 

The bearish argument from is that the highs are losing amplitude, weakening trend, however this can be easily invalidated with a strong new ATH. 

If you want to short IMO you wait for trend + structure break.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: $MU https://t.co/4mNMCgzjsY
tweets.full_text:
$MU 

Smoked earnings last week and the same basic TA has continued to work. 

Draw the trend line, wait for price to retest + demand and then you bid. 

Assume the trend continues until it doesn't. 

The bearish argument from is that the highs are losing amplitude, weakening trend, however this can be easily invalidated with a strong new ATH. 

If you want to short IMO you wait for trend + structure break.

tweets.text:
I haven't claimed they're going bankrupt, sir.

I've talked about the structural risks Saylor and the Strategy team have refused to acknowledge.

They marketed a product as a money market like fund with higher yield and low risk that's still currently trading ~17% beneath par.

They've blatantly broken promises given to investors and rapidly shifted goalposts and key metrics as the previous ones continue to break.

They're shamelessly diluting MSTR holders and have no other viable way of raising cash other than continuing to do so or unload chunks of the BTC they purchased at much higher prices.

Money from new investors is no longer flowing directly into Bitcoin, it's flowing back to the dividends and debt.

BTC has managed to hold $60K, but they've yet to sell a meaningful amount.

We'll see what happens when they do.

Hopefully $60K still holds!

I'd feel much better if today's announcement was that they already sold $1.25B of BTC and $60K held instead of authorizing a future sale of that amount.

Either way - I believe the criticism I've given has been constructive.

BTC may not be down a meaningful amount, but MSTR is still down ~30% even after today's bounce.

And if you've paid attention to my posts, my main encouragement has been for people who are bullish on Bitcoin to rotate back to Bitcoin and hold BTC instead of a levered proxy stock that can be printed on demand.

I'm bullish as hell on the future of the industry, and would not be as passionate as I am sharing what I have about Strategy if I didn't believe it wasn't truly beneficial.
tweets.full_text:
I haven't claimed they're going bankrupt, sir.

I've talked about the structural risks Saylor and the Strategy team have refused to acknowledge.

They marketed a product as a money market like fund with higher yield and low risk that's still currently trading ~17% beneath par.

They've blatantly broken promises given to investors and rapidly shifted goalposts and key metrics as the previous ones continue to break.

They're shamelessly diluting MSTR holders and have no other viable way of raising cash other than continuing to do so or unload chunks of the BTC they purchased at much higher prices.

Money from new investors is no longer flowing directly into Bitcoin, it's flowing back to the dividends and debt.

BTC has managed to hold $60K, but they've yet to sell a meaningful amount.

We'll see what happens when they do.

Hopefully $60K still holds!

I'd feel much better if today's announcement was that they already sold $1.25B of BTC and $60K held instead of authorizing a future sale of that amount.

Either way - I believe the criticism I've given has been constructive.

BTC may not be down a meaningful amount, but MSTR is still down ~30% even after today's bounce.

And if you've paid attention to my posts, my main encouragement has been for people who are bullish on Bitcoin to rotate back to Bitcoin and hold BTC instead of a levered proxy stock that can be printed on demand.

I'm bullish as hell on the future of the industry, and would not be as passionate as I am sharing what I have about Strategy if I didn't believe it wasn't truly beneficial.

tweets.text:
Step in the right direction, but would have felt much better had they announced they actually sold $1.25B worth of BTC and the market was able to hold $60K.

Understand why they sold $1.1B of MSTR, necessary to have some form of dilution right now to raise cash.

But not a fan of how they're pushing the equity buyback program so hard with MSTR, considering it's capped at $1B (less than what they sold in the same announcement), and there's no viable path to execute it.

And also still left the door wide open for selling more MSTR at levels at or near 1 mNAV (last tweet in the thread).

So overall feels like hollow signaling, which is a great marketing tactic and might let them squeeze a bit more from MSTR at higher prices which I assume is their preferred path to continue raising cash before they're actually forced to sell BTC.
tweets.full_text:

But not a fan of how they're pushing the equity buyback program so hard with MSTR, considering it's capped at $1B (less than what they sold in the same announcement), and there's no viable path to execute it.

And also still left the door wide open for selling more MSTR at levels at or near 1 mNAV (last tweet in the thread).

So overall feels like hollow signaling, which is a great marketing tactic and might let them squeeze a bit more from MSTR at higher prices which I assume is their preferred path to continue raising cash before they're actually forced to sell BTC.

tweets.text:
I still remember Token 2049 week in October of 2023 like it was yesterday.

Entire market was at peak fear, and as dead as the timeline felt the vibes in Singapore were electric.

Happened to mark the pico-bottom before the next bull run.

Token 2049 + Sui BaseCamp + F1 week is a no brainer if you're able to make it out there.

Easily one of the best ways to position yourself in a bear market for the next bull market.

tweets.text:
$STRC snapshot for the next dividend is officially tomorrow, but it's effectively today.

Trade has to settle by the time it's taken, and because US markets settle t+1, which means you generally need to have bought by the close of trading on the business day before the ex-dividend/record date.

e.g. why there's typically a small selloff on the snapshot dates, which I'd be surprised isn't amplified to the degree of the recent volatility
tweets.quoted_tweet_text: If you like $STRC at $95, you'll love it at $75. https://t.co/EVbbV5xVET
tweets.full_text:
$STRC snapshot for the next dividend is officially tomorrow, but it's effectively today.

Trade has to settle by the time it's taken, and because US markets settle t+1, which means you generally need to have bought by the close of trading on the business day before the ex-dividend/record date.

e.g. why there's typically a small selloff on the snapshot dates, which I'd be surprised isn't amplified to the degree of the recent volatility

tweets.text:
first of all thankfully no extremely terrible defensive mistakes cus i think thats what cat was most worried about killing this game, danilo and tanaka had errors that technically, led to goal, but i didnt think they were super egregious

first half, even without sano's goal is what cat thought would look like on paper for the 2 systems clashing (but maybe if brazil actually had a proper chance too first half)

the early second half chaos was really bad for japan cus thats where individual flair brazil has did really well and scored equalizer, japan settled back into game, but then appear to have lost idea attacking, maybe they all spent their energy closing in on brazil first half, i thought crossing was a great strategy even though brazil didnt have great advantage in that cus japan was just doing low block bs which is really hard to penetrate

big 2 individual skill cat really enjoyed to shoutout is definitely ueda + vinicius junior

- ueda had mogged the brazil center back repeated on long balls, goal kicks, sometime just directly controlling them, but lacked players to support him after first touch, and his position was so dangerous when he receives these that had any japanese player ran to him it woudlve generated a chance

- vinicius junior, obviously had managed to beat defenders a few times, he is so great at dribbling, but more importantly when he was failing to beat defenders, he had usually drawn 2 defenders, where his pass back to teammate was open to cross, this lead to goal for brazil, and another dangerous one later on
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
sorry if i doubted you sano, high iq interception positioning before goal

i wonder if japan can keep up this level of passion second half when theyre defending spaces cus brazil is completely choked first half, for now, theyre mostly shooting from very far

When I took over Wolf Game, I wanted to build a true GameFi title - something centered around meaningful decisions with real rewards, and a deeper gamified DeFi experience

With onchain resources and PLUMS, a 1:1 USDC wrapper, we’re starting to realize that vision

More to come
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Seeing community members like @cryptdk2 build tools for Wolf Game is incredibly uplifting.

You can now view estimated daily PLUMS output (1 PLUMS = $1) for each resource in each community.

Right now, C69 is estimated at 1.49 PLUMS/ day.

A floor G2 Sheep costs $23.35. At https://t.co/Fnh2ia8ikc
